LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ADRIATIC INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS RICHARD BELTRAME AND DART LIMOUSINES, LLC. IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO CURE THESE DEFICIENCIES. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-18-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS SPIELBAGER OF THE SPIELBAGER LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. 1-408-213-7440 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARC R. JACOBS & ATTY. BENJAMIN HORWITZ OF MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-882-7770 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT ADRIATIC INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Richard Beltrame and Dart Limousines, LLC is sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action and with leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiffs may amend their pleading to allege a rescission claim based on Insurance Code 1764.1 and an alternative declaratory relief claim. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order based on the transcript of proceedings and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Benjamin Horwitz of the law offices of Michelman & Robinson LLP (415.882.7770) appears as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ADRIATIC INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Richard Beltrame and Dart Limousines, LLC. The complaint fails to adequately state claims for breach of contract and violation of the Unfair Competition Law. As to the first cause of action, plaintiffs do not attach or describe in their complaint the terms of the contract at issue. Moreover, both of plaintiffs' claims are based, at least in part, on Adriatic's failure to register with the California Secretary of State. Failing to register and refusing to compromise a lien do not, without more, violate the UCL. There is no specific violation of law alleged and no allegation that Adriatic's conduct is unfair or fraudulent. If they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to cure these deficiencies. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
